# openshift_cluster_aws (Resource)

Creates an OpenShift cluster on AWS using openshift-install IPI. Automatically provisions all AWS infrastructure (VPC, subnets, ELBs, Route53, EC2). Blocks until the cluster is fully installed (~40 min).



## Example Usage

### Minimal — standard RHCOS AMI resolved automatically

`openshift-install` looks up the correct RHCOS AMI for the requested version and region. All AWS infrastructure (VPC, subnets, Route53, ELBs) is created automatically.

```hcl
resource "openshift_cluster_aws" "prod" {
  cluster_name = "prod"
  base_domain  = "example.com"
  region       = "us-east-1"

  pull_secret    = var.pull_secret
  ssh_public_key = var.ssh_public_key

  aws_access_key_id     = var.aws_access_key_id
  aws_secret_access_key = var.aws_secret_access_key

  openshift_version = "4.16"
  worker_replicas   = 3
}

output "console_url" {
  value = openshift_cluster_aws.prod.console_url
}

output "kubeadmin_password" {
  value     = openshift_cluster_aws.prod.kubeadmin_password
  sensitive = true
}
```

### With a custom pre-baked RHCOS AMI

Build the AMI once with `openshift_node_ami`, then reference its `ami_id`. New nodes launch with all container images already cached — no registry pull at boot time. To roll a version upgrade, update `ocp_version` on the `openshift_node_ami` resource; Terraform builds a new AMI and updates the cluster.

```hcl
resource "openshift_node_ami" "worker" {
  ocp_version = "4.16"
  region      = "us-east-1"

  pull_secret           = var.pull_secret
  aws_access_key_id     = var.aws_access_key_id
  aws_secret_access_key = var.aws_secret_access_key
}

resource "openshift_cluster_aws" "prod" {
  cluster_name = "prod"
  base_domain  = "example.com"
  region       = "us-east-1"

  pull_secret    = var.pull_secret
  ssh_public_key = var.ssh_public_key

  aws_access_key_id     = var.aws_access_key_id
  aws_secret_access_key = var.aws_secret_access_key

  openshift_version = "4.16"
  ami_id            = openshift_node_ami.worker.ami_id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `aws_access_key_id` (String, Sensitive) AWS access key ID.
- `aws_secret_access_key` (String, Sensitive) AWS secret access key.
- `base_domain` (String) Base DNS domain. Must be a Route53 public hosted zone in the target AWS account.
- `cluster_name` (String) Name of the cluster. Used as a prefix for all AWS resources.
- `pull_secret` (String, Sensitive) Red Hat pull secret JSON.
- `region` (String) AWS region, e.g. us-east-1.
- `ssh_public_key` (String, Sensitive) SSH public key for cluster nodes.

### Optional

- `additional_trust_bundle` (String) PEM-encoded CA bundle for disconnected/proxy setups.
- `ami_id` (String) Custom RHCOS AMI ID to use for cluster nodes. Use openshift_node_ami to build a pre-baked AMI and reference its ami_id here. If not set, openshift-install resolves the default RHCOS AMI for the OCP version.
- `cluster_network_cidr` (String) CIDR for the cluster pod network.
- `control_plane_instance_type` (String) EC2 instance type for control plane nodes.
- `fips` (Boolean) Enable FIPS mode.
- `install_binary` (String) Path to the openshift-install binary.
- `install_dir` (String) Working directory for openshift-install. Must persist between apply and destroy.
- `install_timeout` (String) Timeout for the install operation (e.g. 90m, 2h).
- `machine_network_cidr` (String) CIDR for the machine (node) network.
- `openshift_version` (String) OpenShift version to install, e.g. '4.14'.
- `publish` (String) Publish strategy: External (public) or Internal (private cluster).
- `service_network_cidr` (String) CIDR for the service network.
- `worker_instance_type` (String) EC2 instance type for worker nodes.
- `worker_replicas` (Number) Number of worker nodes.

### Read-Only

- `api_url` (String) Cluster API URL.
- `cluster_id` (String) OpenShift cluster ID.
- `console_url` (String) Cluster console URL.
- `infra_id` (String) AWS infrastructure ID prefix used for all provisioned resources.
- `kubeadmin_password` (String, Sensitive) kubeadmin password for the cluster console.
- `kubeconfig` (String, Sensitive) Kubeconfig for the installed cluster.
